title: Scorpions go terminator mode and reinforce their weapons with metal
description: Different hunting patterns seem to dictate different distributions of metal.

title: Pentagon inks deals with Nvidia, Microsoft, and AWS to deploy AI on classified networks
description: The deals come as the DOD has doubled down on diversifying its exposure to AI vendors in the wake of its controversial dispute with Anthropic over usage terms of its AI models.

title: Cyber-Insecurity in the AI Era
description: Cybersecurity was already under strain before AI entered the stack. Now, as AI expands the attack surface and adds new complexity, the limits of legacy approaches are becoming harder to ignore. title: GPT-5.5 matches heavily hyped Mythos Preview in new cybersecurity tests
description: New results suggest Mythos' cyber threat isn't "a breakthrough specific to one model."

title: Operationalizing AI for Scale and Sovereignty
description: Companies are taking control of their own data to tailor AI for their needs. The challenge lies in balancing ownership with the safe, trusted flow of high‑quality data needed to power reliable insights. title: Is your Purosangue SUV not sharp enough? Ferrari has you covered.
description: We'll soon get to see the brand's first EV; first, a more honed V12 four-seater.

title: Virgin Galactic reveals new ship, but it's running out of time and cash
description: It's not clear whether Virgin Galactic has the cash reserves to fund a prolonged test phase.

title: Apple may take "several months" to catch up to Mac mini and Studio demand
description: Chip shortages and demand from AI enthusiasts are both playing a part.

title: The Download: a new Christian phone network, and debugging LLMs
description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. title: Inexpensive seafloor-hopping submersibles could stoke deep-sea science—and mining
description: Smack dab between Australia and South America, the US National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) research vessel Rainier is currently on a mission to map more than 8,000 square nautical miles of the Pacific seafloor in search of critical mineral deposits. title: Trump’s mass firing just dealt another blow to American science
description: This past week delivered another gut punch for science in the US. This time, the target was the National Science Foundation—a federal agency that funds major research projects to the tune of around $9 billion. title: ChatGPT Images 2.0 is a hit in India, but not a big winner elsewhere, yet
description: Users in India are embracing ChatGPT Images 2.0 for creative, personal visuals — from avatars to cinematic portraits.

title: How Shivon Zilis Operated as Elon Musk’s OpenAI Insider
description: Messages presented at trial reveal how Zilis, the mother of four of Musk’s children, acted as an intermediary between him and OpenAI.

title: Sources: Anthropic potential $900B+ valuation round could happen within 2 weeks
description: Anthropic is asking investors to submit allocations for the AI company’s latest fundraise within the next 48 hours, according to sources familiar with the matter.

title: Good Luck Getting a Mac Mini for the Next ‘Several Months’
description: Apple CEO Tim Cook told analysts that AI adoption has happened faster than expected.

title: Apple was surprised by AI-driven demand for Macs
description: Apple said it will be supply-constrained on Mac mini, Studio, and Neo in the next quarter, too.

title: Legal AI startup Legora hits $5.6B valuation and its battle with Harvey just got hotter
description: The two wildly fast-growing rivals have raised massive sums, pushed into each other's home turf, and now have dueling ad campaigns.

title: Musk v. Altman Kicks Off, DOJ Guts Voting Rights Unit, and Is the AI Job Apocalypse Overhyped?
description: In this episode of Uncanny Valley, we get into how the Elon Musk-Sam Altman trial goes way beyond their rivalry and could have major implications both for OpenAI and also the AI industry at large.

title: Elon Musk Seemingly Admits xAI Has Used OpenAI’s Models to Train Its Own
description: While answering questions under oath, Musk argued it’s standard practice for AI labs to use their competitors’ models.

title: OpenAI Rolls Out ‘Advanced’ Security Mode for At-Risk Accounts
description: OpenAI is rolling out Advanced Account Security for people concerned that their ChatGPT or Codex accounts could be potential targets of phishing attacks.

description: Understanding the building blocks and design choices of graph neural networks.

title: A Gentle Introduction to Graph Neural Networks
description: What components are needed for building learning algorithms that leverage the structure and properties of graphs?

title: Distill Hiatus
description: After five years, Distill will be taking a break.

title: Adversarial Reprogramming of Neural Cellular Automata
description: Reprogramming Neural CA to exhibit novel behaviour, using adversarial attacks.

title: Weight Banding
description: Weights in the final layer of common visual models appear as horizontal bands. We investigate how and why.
